subject: An explosive burst of magma and fire erupting outward from a central point, framed against a deep red sky
description: A roiling, radial blast of molten fire fills the frame — bright yellow-white at the core fading through orange and deep red at the edges. The eruption has a sun-burst or supernova quality, with jagged tongues of flame and incandescent embers radiating outward. The background is a sky of saturated reds and oranges, suggesting either an intense sunset or a fire-choked desert horizon. There are no figures, no architecture, and no discrete objects — the entire composition is the elemental event itself. The art reads as pure abstract violence: heat, eruption, immolation.
foreground: radial burst of molten fire and embers, white-hot core fading to orange
background: saturated red-orange sky, possibly desert horizon obscured by heat
emotion: violent, sudden, destructive — the image of a flashpoint
